root_cause.text: The code fails to halt message processing when the message size exceeds the maximum allowed limit, continuing to publish the content header even after initiating the channel closure.
root_cause.evidence: In `AMQChannel.java`, the call to `publishContentHeader(new ContentHeaderBody(properties, bodySize))` was executed unconditionally after the size check, and the patch places it inside an `else` block to ensure it is only called if the message size is within the allowed limit.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The code fails to halt message processing when the message size exceeds the maximum allowed limit, continuing to publish the content header even after initiating the channel closure.
agent_logs.evidence_points: In `AMQChannel.java`, the call to `publishContentHeader(new ContentHeaderBody(properties, bodySize))` was executed unconditionally after the size check, and the patch places it inside an `else` block to ensure it is only called if the message size is within the allowed limit.

intent_record.intent_summary: Prevent publishing content headers for messages that exceed the maximum size limit.
intent_record.what: Wrapped publishContentHeader in an else block after the message size check.
intent_record.how: Prevents the publication of the content header when the message is too large and the channel is closed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add an integration test to verify the broker correctly enforces the maximum message size limit.
intent_record.what: Adds a new test class MaximumMessageSize to verify broker behavior when the maximum message size limit is exceeded.
intent_record.how: Introduces an integration test that configures a maximum message size of 1000, sends a message of size 1001, and asserts that the broker rejects the message and closes the channel.

grouping_result.intent: Prevent publishing content headers for messages that exceed the maximum size limit.
Add an integration test to verify the broker correctly enforces the maximum message size limit.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by preventing the publishing of content headers for messages that exceed the maximum size limit, fixing the exact issue described.
The group adds an integration test specifically designed to verify the correct enforcement of the maximum message size limit, which directly addresses the root cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: The code continues to publish the content header here even after the channel closure is initiated.
The code fails to halt execution (e.g., missing a return statement) after initiating channel closure.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement is improperly executed after a message-too-large error is detected and the channel is instructed to close, because the preceding code lacks a control flow break (like a return statement).
This initiates the channel closure but fails to return, allowing execution to improperly continue.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: QPID-6125 : [Java Broker] AMQP 0-8/9/9-1 protocol handler refactoring
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e91f279199ac5419d71ba7b15070c585f4962eb3
Author: Robert Godfrey <rgodfrey@apache.org>
Date:   Fri Oct 17 14:23:19 2014 +0000

    QPID-6125 : [Java Broker] AMQP 0-8/9/9-1 protocol handler refactoring

diff --git a/broker-plugins/amqp-0-8-protocol/src/main/java/org/apache/qpid/server/protocol/v0_8/AMQChannel.java b/broker-plugins/amqp-0-8-protocol/src/main/java/org/apache/qpid/server/protocol/v0_8/AMQChannel.java
index f6ef4256d0..5ca94891c1 100644
--- a/broker-plugins/amqp-0-8-protocol/sr
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `receiveMessageHeader` processes message headers but does not halt processing when the message size exceeds limits or when the channel is already closing. It calls `publishContentHeader(new ContentHeaderBody(properties, bodySize));` without checking if the header should be processed based on maximum size constraints or channel state, matching the vulnerability description.

index 9d92303ce9..0bef451c06 100644
--- a/broker-plugins/amqp-0-8-protocol/src/main/java/org/apache/qpid/s
bic_trace_step.reason: The receiveContentHeader method checks if the bodySize exceeds the maximum allowed limit and calls closeChannel() if it does, but it does not return or halt execution. It continues to call publishContentHeader() immediately after, matching the described vulnerability.
